title: Air Fryer Roast Potatoes Recipe
summary: Discover the ultimate way to enjoy crispy, golden-brown potatoes with our air fryer roast potatoes recipe. Perfect for a Sunday dinner or as a delightful side for any meal, this recipe leverages the power of the air fryer to deliver perfectly roasted potatoes with minimal oil. Using white potatoes or red potatoes, this method ensures a satisfying crunch on the outside while keeping the inside tender and fluffy. Whether you’re pairing these with roast chicken or serving them alongside steak, the air fryer’s convection cooking mimics traditional oven roasting but with less time and fuss. Ideal for busy home cooks looking to enhance their side dishes, our recipe transforms humble potatoes into a gourmet treat, perfect for Christmas dinner or a regular weeknight meal. Enjoy these crispy fries without the extra calories from deep-frying!

method.description: Peel the potatoes and cut into chunky roast potato shapes. Transfer to a pan of cold water with a big pinch of salt. Put on the lid, bring to the boil and simmer for 7 mins.
Drain well in a colander, tossing well to rough up the edges. At this stage you could spread them out on a baking tray to cool, then chill for up to 24 hrs until needed.
Remove the needles from the rosemary and very finely chop. Scrape into a bowl with the flaky sea salt and finely grate in the zest from the lemon.
When ready to cook, tip the potatoes into a bowl and spoon in 3 tbsp of the fat or oil, gently tossing to coat. Sprinkle some of the rosemary salt on top and gently toss.

Fit the air fryer basket with a tray, or make your own with foil. Tip the potatoes onto the tray, spreading them out so they’re as close to a single layer as possible. Drizzle with the remaining 2 tbsp fat or oil and sprinkle with more rosemary salt.
Heat the air fryer to 200°C and put the basket full of potatoes in. Set the timer to 45 mins. After 20 mins or so, toss the potatoes really well, and roast until super crunchy and crisp.
Tip into a bowl and sprinkle with a bit of the remaining rosemary salt, if you like.

chefs.name: Adam Bush
chefs.summary: Adam is a chef, baker and food writer with a love of big, bold flavours. He trained in Michelin-starred kitchens, pubs and a bakery before turning his hand to recipe writing.
